subject: Contract renewal — Lowlands Dairy
body:
Hi Pieter,

Happy new year. As you know our contract with Lowlands expired December 31. We're operating month-to-month for now, but I'd like to get the renewal discussion started properly. Can we schedule a call this month?

David

subject: RE: Contract renewal — Lowlands Dairy
body:
David,

Good to hear from you, and happy new year. Yes, renewal is on our radar. Before we go to formal terms I need to flag that our production planning team has been reviewing outbound commitments and we expect changes on the volume side. Happy to discuss on a call — how does January 22 look?

One thing to note before we get into terms: please be aware that all Lowlands Dairy pricing is denominated in EUR. Strauss is responsible for any currency conversion costs; the applicable rate is that of the payment date, not the order date. We have had confusion on this with other customers so I want to be explicit from the start.

Pieter

subject: Renewal framework — Lowlands Dairy proposal
body:
David,

Good call earlier. As discussed, here is our proposed framework for the 2026 contract:

- Term: 18 months
- Volume: minimum 25 MT/quarter, maximum 40 MT/quarter
- Price: €3,350/ton
- Payment: Net 30

The key change from the old contract is the minimum commitment — we have moved it to 25 MT from 20 MT. This reflects new production scheduling requirements. We cannot hold capacity for a customer at 20 MT minimum anymore.

I have also been instructed by our finance committee to raise two commercial points for the renewal:

First, for the renewed contract volumes, our finance committee requires payment by irrevocable Letter of Credit denominated in EUR, issued by a correspondent bank acceptable to our co-op, no later than 30 days before each shipment date. We can discuss whether this applies to all shipments or only those above a threshold volume.

Second, we would like to move to net 60 payment terms for the renewal. Current net 30 is difficult for our co-op cash flow given the 4-6 week cross-border SEPA transfer timelines on the Israel route. This is something we would like to raise as a negotiation point, not a demand.

I have cc'd Anja Dekker, our volume planning lead.

Pieter

subject: RE: Renewal framework — Lowlands Dairy proposal
body:
Pieter,

Thanks for the framework. A few reactions:

On price — €3,350 is a significant step up from the old contract. Can you share what is driving that number?

On minimum volume — 25 MT vs 20 MT is something I need to check internally. Our Q4 2025 actual offtake was around 19 MT so committing to a 25 MT floor is not straightforward.

On the Letter of Credit — we have never operated on LC terms with Lowlands. This adds administrative burden and cost on our side. I will need to understand what is driving the finance committee's position before I can respond.

On net 60 — we operate on net 30 with most suppliers. I understand the cash flow argument but this would be a change we would need to evaluate carefully.

I'll come back with a counter by end of next week.

David

subject: Q1 delivery — January/February
body:
David,

This is Anja from planning. While renewal is being discussed we are continuing to process your monthly orders under the old terms. The January shipment of 7 MT left January 31, ETA at your site February 6.

For February we have you down for 7 MT around February 20 — please confirm this is still needed.

Anja

subject: RE: Q1 delivery — confirmed
body:
Confirmed, February 7 MT on the 20th is fine. Thanks Anja.

David

subject: Counter-proposal — renewal terms
body:
Pieter,

Our counter on the renewal framework:

- Term: 18 months (agreed)
- Volume: minimum 20 MT/quarter, maximum 40 MT/quarter (we need to hold the original minimum)
- Price: €3,250/ton
- Payment: Net 30 (we cannot move to net 60 — our AP processes are built around 30-day cycles and this would require a policy exception I am unlikely to get approved)

On the Letter of Credit — we would propose to maintain open account terms for shipments under 25 MT/quarter, with LC applicable only for shipments above that threshold. This limits the administrative burden while addressing your finance committee's concern for larger volumes.

David

subject: RE: Counter-proposal
body:
David,

Thank you for the counter. On price — we could consider €3,280 but not lower. Input costs simply do not allow it.

On minimum volume — 20 MT is not workable. The co-op voted on this. We could consider 23 MT as a compromise.

On the LC — your proposal on the threshold is reasonable. We can take that back to the finance committee.

On net 60 — we accept that you cannot move to 60 days. We will drop this request.

Still some gap to close. Can we get on a call this week?

Pieter

subject: Following up — renewal status
body:
Pieter,

We have been going back and forth since January and are still not aligned on price and volume minimum. I want to make sure we don't drift further on an informal basis without a signed contract.

Where do things stand? Are you still at 23 MT minimum / €3,280?

David

subject: RE: Renewal status
body:
David,

Yes, we are still at those positions. I have had our legal team put together a draft contract document — sending separately. We would like to have something signed before end of April.

Pieter

subject: Draft contract — open items
body:
Pieter,

We have reviewed the draft. The main issues remain: minimum volume (we reviewed internally and 20 MT is our limit) and the price (we can go to €3,260 but not €3,280).

I know we have been at an impasse on these for a while. Is there any flexibility on your side, or do you need to go back to the co-op?

David

subject: Q2 delivery schedule
body:
David,

Sending Q2 delivery schedule as we continue on the informal basis:
- April 28: 7 MT
- May 19: 7 MT
- June 10: 6 MT
Total Q2: 20 MT. Please confirm if these dates work.

Note from Pieter: renewal discussion still ongoing, he will follow up separately.

Anja

subject: Renewal — still open
body:
David,

I owe you an update. I brought the 20 MT minimum back to our board and they will not approve it. The co-op's position is firm at 23 MT. On price we could meet at €3,270.

I know this is frustrating. We have been very patient operating without a signed contract but this cannot continue indefinitely. What is Strauss's final position?

Pieter

subject: RE: Renewal — Strauss position
body:
Pieter,

I have escalated internally. Here is where we land: we can move to 22 MT minimum as a compromise and €3,270 on price. That is our final position.

On the LC — can you confirm the finance committee's decision on our threshold proposal (open account below 25 MT, LC above)? That needs to be resolved before we can sign.

David
